title: Brachypterum eriocarpum

It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Brachypterum eriocarpum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Brachypterum eriocarpum is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used to treat digestive issues and skin conditions.
